# ThaiHelper — Complete Platform Guide

> ThaiHelper (https://thaihelper.app) is Thailand's first free, direct-hire marketplace for household staff. No agency, no middleman — families and helpers connect directly.

## Who Is ThaiHelper For?

### Helpers / Job Seekers
ThaiHelper is built for domestic workers and household professionals in Thailand who want to find work directly with families, without going through an expensive agency. This includes:
- **Nannies & Babysitters** — caring for children of all ages
- **Housekeepers & Cleaners** — daily or weekly cleaning, laundry, organizing
- **Private Chefs & Cooks** — preparing meals for families (Thai, Western, international cuisine)
- **Drivers & Chauffeurs** — school runs, errands, airport transfers, personal driving
- **Gardeners & Pool Care** — garden maintenance, landscaping, pool cleaning
- **Elder Caregivers** — companionship and care for elderly family members
- **Tutors & Teachers** — English tutoring, Thai language, math, music, and more

Registration is **100% free for helpers**. No sign-up fee, no monthly fee, no commission taken from salary. Helpers set their own rates, schedule, and preferences.

### Families / Employers
ThaiHelper serves families living in Thailand who need household help:
- **Expat families** from Europe, USA, UK, Australia, Russia, and other countries
- **Thai families** looking for verified, reliable household staff
- **Digital nomads** who need part-time or short-term help
- **Retirees** who need companionship, driving, or home care
- **Business owners** who need personal staff

Families can browse helper profiles for free. To contact helpers directly, families register as employers.

## Cities Covered

ThaiHelper covers all of Thailand, with the strongest presence in:

### Bangkok (กรุงเทพฯ)
The largest market. Expat families in Sukhumvit, Sathorn, Silom, and Thonglor areas frequently hire nannies, housekeepers, and drivers. Bangkok salaries are the highest in Thailand.

### Chiang Mai (เชียงใหม่)
Popular with digital nomads and retirees. Strong demand for English-speaking nannies and housekeepers. More affordable than Bangkok.

### Phuket (ภูเก็ต)
Tourist and expat hub. High demand for pool care, housekeepers, private chefs, and drivers. Salaries similar to Bangkok.

### Pattaya (พัทยา)
Large retiree community. Demand for elder caregivers, drivers, and housekeepers. Mid-range salaries.

### Koh Samui (เกาะสมุย)
Island living means higher demand for self-contained household staff. Private chefs and housekeepers are especially sought after.

### Hua Hin (หัวหิน)
Retirement and weekend destination. Steady demand for housekeepers and elder caregivers.

## Detailed Salary Data (2026)

### Full-Time Nanny (Live-Out, Mon-Sat)
| City | Low | Mid | High |
|------|-----|-----|------|
| Bangkok | 15,000 | 20,000 | 25,000+ THB |
| Chiang Mai | 12,000 | 15,000 | 18,000 THB |
| Phuket | 15,000 | 18,000 | 22,000 THB |
| Pattaya | 13,000 | 16,000 | 20,000 THB |
| Koh Samui | 14,000 | 17,000 | 20,000 THB |

### Full-Time Housekeeper (Live-Out)
| City | Low | Mid | High |
|------|-----|-----|------|
| Bangkok | 12,000 | 15,000 | 18,000 THB |
| Chiang Mai | 10,000 | 13,000 | 16,000 THB |
| Phuket | 12,000 | 15,000 | 18,000 THB |
| Pattaya | 10,000 | 13,000 | 16,000 THB |

### Part-Time Housekeeper (2-3 days/week)
| City | Low | High |
|------|-----|------|
| Bangkok | 4,000 | 8,000 THB |
| Other cities | 3,000 | 6,000 THB |

### Full-Time Driver
| City | Low | Mid | High |
|------|-----|-----|------|
| Bangkok | 15,000 | 20,000 | 25,000 THB |
| Other cities | 12,000 | 16,000 | 20,000 THB |

### Private Chef (Full-Time)
| Experience | Salary Range |
|-----------|-------------|
| Thai cuisine only | 15,000 – 22,000 THB |
| International cuisine | 20,000 – 35,000 THB |
| Hotel/restaurant trained | 25,000 – 45,000+ THB |

### Elder Caregiver
| Type | Salary Range |
|------|-------------|
| Part-time | 8,000 – 12,000 THB |
| Full-time (live-out) | 13,000 – 20,000 THB |
| Full-time (live-in) | 12,000 – 18,000 THB |

## Legal Information

### Thai Labor Law for Domestic Workers
Domestic workers in Thailand are protected under **Ministerial Regulation No. 14 (B.E. 2555/2012)**, which extends key labor protections:

- **Minimum wage**: Provincial rates apply (370 THB/day in Bangkok, 330-370 in other provinces as of 2026)
- **Rest days**: Minimum 1 day per week
- **Annual leave**: 6 paid days after 1 year
- **Public holidays**: 13 paid holidays per year
- **Social Security**: Mandatory registration for full-time employees (Section 33) — 5% employer + 5% employee contribution
- **Severance pay**: Required for dismissal without cause (30-300 days depending on tenure)

### Work Permits for Foreign Helpers
Helpers from Myanmar, Laos, and Cambodia need a valid work permit (MOU system or border pass). Filipino helpers need both a work permit and a visa. The employer is legally responsible for ensuring proper documentation.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Is ThaiHelper free?**
A: Yes, it is 100% free for helpers. Employers pay a subscription to access helper contact details.

**Q: Is ThaiHelper a recruitment agency?**
A: No. ThaiHelper is a marketplace platform. We do not employ, place, or guarantee workers. Families and helpers connect directly and make their own arrangements.

**Q: How is ThaiHelper different from Facebook groups?**
A: ThaiHelper offers verified profiles with structured information (experience, skills, languages, photos, reviews). Facebook groups have no verification, no profiles, and are full of spam.

**Q: How is ThaiHelper different from traditional agencies?**
A: Agencies charge 1-3 months' salary as a placement fee and often take a cut from the helper's pay. ThaiHelper has zero fees for helpers and connects families directly with staff.

**Q: Can I find English-speaking helpers?**
A: Yes. Many helpers on ThaiHelper speak English, especially those who have worked with expat families. You can filter by language on the platform.

**Q: What cities does ThaiHelper cover?**
A: All of Thailand. The strongest presence is in Bangkok, Chiang Mai, Phuket, Pattaya, Koh Samui, and Hua Hin.

**Q: How do I know if a helper is trustworthy?**
A: ThaiHelper verifies helper IDs. Profiles include work history, skills, references, and (soon) reviews from previous employers.

**Q: Can I hire a helper for a short-term job?**
A: Yes. Many helpers are available for one-time, weekly, or short-term arrangements. You can filter by availability on the platform.

## Platform Features

- **Verified profiles** — ID-checked helpers and employers
- **Multilingual** — Available in English, Thai, and Russian
- **City-based search** — Find helpers in your specific city or area
- **Category filtering** — Search by nanny, housekeeper, chef, driver, etc.
- **Experience filtering** — Find helpers with the right level of experience
- **Language filtering** — Find helpers who speak your language
- **Direct messaging** — Contact helpers directly, no intermediary
- **Mobile-friendly** — Works on any device
- **Free for helpers** — Always and forever
